content.text:
```mdl
@ Confidentiality (Non‑Disclosure) Agreement – Model
module contract_nli_no_align_dev_12_nda_4

open std.strings

-- ----------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Domain Types
-- ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Parties to the agreement
type Party = {
    name: string
}

@ Purpose for which Confidential Information may be disclosed
type Purpose =
    | AgreementPurpose(unit)   -- the purpose expressly stated in the Agreement
    | OtherPurpose(unit)       -- any purpose not covered by the Agreement

@ Confidential Information as defined in Sections 1‑3
type ConfidentialInformation = {
    disclosed_by: Party,      -- the Party that provides the information
    received_by: Party,       -- the Party that receives the information
    purpose: Purpose,         -- intended use of the information
    content: string           -- abstract representation of the data
}

-- ----------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Entities (public model objects)
-- ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ The disclosing Party (CLIENT)
entity client: Party

@ The receiving Party (BROOKS)
entity brooks: Party

@ Each piece of Confidential Information exchanged under the Agreement
entity confidential_info: ConfidentialInformation

-- ----------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Helper Functions
-- ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Determine whether a purpose is the one expressly allowed by the Agreement
func is_agreement_purpose(p: Purpose) -> bool:
    case p:
        | Purpose.AgreementPurpose(): true
        | Purpose.OtherPurpose(): false

-- ----------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Obligations / Prohibitions (rules)
-- ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Clause 1.2 – BROOKS must not disclose Confidential Information to anyone
@           outside BROOKS or to any employee without a need‑to‑know.
rule F disclose_confidential_information:
    confidential_info.received_by = brooks and
    not (confidential_info.disclosed_by = brooks) always

@ Clause 2.2 – BROOKS acknowledges that breach causes irreparable harm.
rule O injunctive_relief:
    confidential_info.received_by = brooks always

@ Clause 3 – Exceptions (already possessed, independently developed, etc.).
@ The model treats these as separate entities; no rule is needed here.

@ Clause 4 – Compelled disclosure: BROOKS must notify CLIENT before any
@           legally compelled disclosure.
rule O notify_before_compelled_disclosure:
    confidential_info.received_by = brooks and
    /* The act of being legally compelled is an external event; we model the
       obligation to notify as a requirement that the notification occurs
       before the disclosure. */
    true always   -- placeholder – actual event modeling omitted for brevity

@ Clause 5 – Return of material after termination.
rule O return_material_upon_termination:
    /* The termination event is not modeled; we require that the material
       be returned (or retained for 3 years) after termination. */
    true always   -- placeholder – temporal condition omitted for brevity

@ Clause 6 – Governing law (no normative constraint on entities).

@ Clause 7 – Survival (term of the Agreement).

@ ----------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Hypothesis (derived requirement)
-- ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Receiving Party shall not use any Confidential Information for any purpose
@ other than the purposes stated in the Agreement.
rule F use_confidential_information_for_other_purposes:
    confidential_info.received_by = brooks and
    not is_agreement_purpose(confidential_info.purpose) always
```
